key_insight: Physics-Augmented Neural Operators and Constitutive Artificial Neural Operators learn a direct map from measured displacement/force data to hyperelastic constitutive functions, enabling fast material model discovery without iterative calibration.
engineering_relevance: This offers near-instantaneous, data-driven constitutive identification for solid mechanics workflows, with built-in physical admissibility and improved robustness to noise, missing data, and mesh changes.
method: Encode full-field displacements with Laplacian eigenfunctions for discretization-independent input representation.
Train neural operators on simulated displacement–reaction-force tuples to infer strain-energy density functions.
Constrain outputs to physically admissible hyperelastic models via physics augmentation or constitutive parameterization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports testing on unseen, noisy, incomplete, differently discretized, and differently sized geometries to assess generalization.
watch_item: Performance depends on the representativeness of the simulated training set, so extrapolation to material behaviors or loading regimes not seen in training may be unreliable.
paper_title: Neural operators solve inverse problems for constitutive model discovery
